meaning: vi.离开,起程,不按照,逝世adj.过去的,逝世的
examples: They depart, with a smile and a nod, and we miss them, and feel resentful.
The research showed that the airport you depart from also influences the cost.
These vehicles are only rated to spend about 200 days in space, so they'll have to depart soon.
Later in the afternoon, the President will depart Portsmouth, New Hampshire en route Boston, Massachusetts.
It was Christmas Eve, and the entire village had turned out to watch the hunters depart.
